content.title: Mxx-keyed materials fidelity
content.learner_task: Work through every material in order.

M1.content:
RF-M1-LONGFORM: inflation measurement distinguishes CPI basket scope from GDP deflator coverage.

| Lens | Household A | Household B |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| Distribution | fixed income | variable income |
| Adaptation | limited | flexible |

M2.content:
RF-M2-WORKED: Stepwise CPI contrast.

1. Define the baskets for CPI and GDP deflator.
2. Compare scope: consumer goods versus all domestic output.
3. Conclude why fixed baskets change interpretation.

M3.content:
RF-M3-CHECKLIST heading

- [ ] RF-M3-check-alpha: I compared basket composition.
- [ ] RF-M3-check-beta: I explained scope differences.

M4.content:
RF-M4-PROMPT-SET lead-in

- RF-M4-prompt-one: How does basket rigidity affect CPI?
- RF-M4-prompt-two: When is GDP deflator more informative?

M5.content:
RF-M5-TEMPLATE: Independent judgement memo scaffold.

**To:** [Recipient]
**From:** [Your Name]
Write your recommendation using explicit criteria.

M6.content: RF-M6-SCENARIO: Maya household faces 5% inflation with fixed monthly income and rising utility costs.

M7.content: RF-M7-CONSOLIDATION: Key ideas include measurement choice, household vulnerability, and criteria-led evaluation.

M8.content: RF-M8-UNKNOWN-FALLBACK: unsupported type must still render this sentence verbatim.

content.title: Array materials fidelity
content.learner_task: Complete both text materials and the checklist.

materials.content: RF-M9-ARRAY-TEXT-ONE: first long-form exposition block must survive normalization.
RF-M10-ARRAY-TEXT-TWO: second long-form exposition block must not be dropped by type bucketing.
- [ ] RF-M11-array-check: I verified both exposition blocks appear in the export.
